purpose: Track propagation of EM stress from frontier to core economies using historical lag patterns, twin-deficit vulnerability filters, and push/pull classification.
components: Historical lag: 3-6 month propagation from frontier to core across four episodes (2013, 2015-16, 2018, 2022)
Twin-deficit filter: CA deficit + fiscal deficit >5% of GDP flags vulnerable core EMs
Push vs pull classification: Fed hiking (push) vs commodity shock (pull) affects severity and speed
Currently flagged core EMs: Turkey, Colombia, Chile
current_assessment: Month 2-4 of predicted cascade sequence. Frontier stress (Egypt, Pakistan, Nigeria) is food-security-driven (atypical vs prior financial-imbalance-driven episodes). Current Fed hold may lengthen lag and reduce severity vs 3/4 prior episodes where Fed was actively hiking.
limitations: Current stress is food-security-driven vs financial-imbalance-driven in prior episodes
Fed hold fundamentally differs from prior hiking episodes
3-6 month lag may not be stable across different shock types
